name: Wool Blend Overcoat
description: A generously-cut wool overcoat with a clean, uncomplicated silhouette — built to layer over everything else I own.
whyThisFitsMyArchive: The cut is generous without looking sloppy, which means I can layer heavily underneath and still look put together. I disappear into the coat, which is the point — the focus stays on where I'm going, not on the coat itself.
whatItReplaces: Doesn't replace anything currently owned — there's no proper winter coat in the wardrobe yet, which is the actual gap this fills.
whyIChooseThis: Over more structured coats: this doesn't require strategic styling to work. Over designer overcoats: same silhouette, a fraction of the price, and one I'll actually wear without worrying about it.
whatItPairsWithInMyCollection: Over the wool trousers, Supima tees, the overshirt. Works over cream, black, navy, dark brown — monochrome layering. Never with a belt; the drape is the point.
layeringCombinations: Over everything — the wool trousers, the crewneck, the turtleneck, the overshirt. It's built to go over the heaviest layering, not compete with it.
colourCombinations: Dark brown is the priority colour — it's warmer and more forgiving than black against the rest of the wardrobe's palette. Black and stone are the alternates.
bestOccasions: The coldest months, formal and casual both
costPerWearReasoning: ₹18,900 is the single biggest number in the wardrobe, and the honest reasoning is that it only pays off if genuinely worn through multiple winters — the reason it's a Dream Piece and not a near-term buy.
whatMakesItTimeless: A generous, simple silhouette that hasn't gone out of style in decades. Wool ages visibly and well.
whatProblemItSolves: One coat that handles autumn through early spring, formal and casual, instead of three separate coats.
whatCouldReplaceIt: Nothing quite matches this silhouette at this price point — it's the reason I chose it.
similarPiecesIWouldProbablyLike: Massimo Dutti wool trousers (same long-term thinking).
layeringOver: the turtleneck, the crewneck, the overshirt — everything else in the wardrobe
alternativeUnder3000: Not realistic for a proper wool overcoat — skip this tier entirely
alternativeUnder10000: Uniqlo Wool Blend Chesterfield Coat (~₹8,990 at uniqlo.com/in) — thinner but same silhouette
buyRecommendation: Wait for Sale — the single biggest opportunity in the wardrobe. This drops to ₹9,000-₹12,000 in end-of-season clearance (February-March). Buy then only.
